channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Judgment Day is coming… for AI
item.description: Artificial intelligence has great potential in predicting outcomes. While AI can predict the likelihood of precipitation, it most likely won’t help you dress or prepare for inclement weather. That preparation involves more than the probability of rain or snow.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sony hikes PlayStation 5 console prices for Europe, UK and Australia citing economic turmoil
item.description: Sony says it will raise prices starting Monday for some PlayStation 5 video game consoles in Europe, Australia and New Zealand, citing global economic turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Groundcover grows funding for eBPF-based observability tech
item.description: When Groundcover entered the observability market in 2021, it was already a mature market with established vendors.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Google’s BigQuery and Looker get agents to simplify analytics tasks
item.description: Google has added new agents to its BigQuery data warehouse and Looker business intelligence platform to help data practitioners automate and simplify analytics tasks.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI fears irreparable harm from Musk, files countersuit
item.description: OpenAI has filed a countersuit against Elon Musk, accusing the billionaire of a sustained campaign to damage the company and urging a US federal court to block further actions it described as unlawful and disruptive.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cutting-edge drone tech maps land and water with laser accuracy
item.description: New drone technology maps land and water with stunning accuracy, giving researchers and conservationists a new way to understand our planet.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Adding smarts to Azure data lakes with Fabric Data Agents
item.description: Enterprise AI needs one thing if it’s to get around the limitations of large language models and deliver the results businesses need from their agents.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OPM Digitizes Federal Retirement Booklets
item.description: Federal annuitants can now access digital versions of key retirement booklets online, a move that brings the Office of Personnel Management closer to its goal of modernizing the federal retirement process.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: Google adds natural language query capabilities to AlloyDB
item.description: Google is enhancing AlloyDB, its fully managed database-as-a-service (DBaaS), to help developers build applications underpinned by generative AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Broadcom favors stock buyback over investing in innovation
item.description: Broadcom plans to spend its cash pile on buying back as much as $10 billion worth of its common stock, dampening IT department hopes that it might invest in innovation or make its products more affordable.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Android feature prevents sensitive content in Google Messages
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about how Google kept Android SafetyCore details quiet until it scanned users' photos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
